extra.reasoning: The property requires rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b to never be jointly true. rec_drug_b is set true by r1 whenever renal_impairment is true. rec_drug_a is set true by r2 whenever its 10-way conjunction holds, and that conjunction explicitly includes renal_impairment. Thus r2's firing condition is a subset of r1's firing condition: any input that fires r2 also fires r1. The two rules target different outputs (rec_drug_a vs rec_drug_b), so priorities and conflict resolution do not suppress either—both fire together. Setting renal_impairment, hyperkalemia, heart_failure, seizure_history, peptic_ulcer, bradycardia, qt_prolongation, low_ejection_fraction, immunosuppressed, and hypertension all true makes both rec_drug_a (via r2) and rec_drug_b (via r1) true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. The remaining inputs (asthma, anticoagulated, polypharmacy) only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; set false here.
